strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 validates qty with the single check `!Number.isInteger(item.qty) || item.qty <= 0`, whereas P1 adds a redundant `typeof item.qty !== 'number'` guard that Number.isInteger already subsumes, making P1's condition needlessly duplicated.

P2 computes the next id via `items.reduce((maxId, item) => (typeof item.id === 'number' && Number.isFinite(item.id) ? Math.max(maxId, item.id) : maxId), 0) + 1`, guarding against non-numeric ids, while P1's `Math.max(0, ...items.map((item) => item.id)) + 1` spreads the entire items array as call arguments (a stack-overflow risk on large lists) and yields NaN if any existing item.id is not numeric.

P1 adds a third test, 'rejects a non-array items value without changing the list', directly exercising the spec-mandated `invalid_body` behavior, while P2's diff only adds the two minimum-required tests and never asserts the non-array/invalid_body path at all.

P2's `post()` test helper explicitly sets `'Content-Length': Buffer.byteLength(payload)`, making the HTTP request construction self-contained and explicit, whereas P1's `post()` sends only a `content-type` header and relies on Node's implicit chunked-encoding default for the body.
